text_a: Introduction
label: O

text_a: Sexual differentiation generates anatomical , physiological , and behavioral dimorphisms that are essential for sexual reproduction .
label: O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: Many of these dimorphisms affect somatic cells , but the sexual dimorphisms that most directly mediate sexual reproduction are those of the gametes themselves .
label: O O O O O O B-CL O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-CL O O

text_a: Gametes differ between the sexes in size and morphology , sometimes dramatically so , reflecting their very different roles in zygote formation .
label: B-CL O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-CL O O

text_a: Indeed , the morphology of the gametes is what defines sex : females are the sex that produces the larger gametes and males produce the smaller ones .
label: O O O O O O B-CL O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-CL O O O O O O O

text_a: Mammalian meiosis is regulated sex - specifically starting in embryogenesis and continuing through much of adult life ( reviewed in [ 1 ] ) .
label: B-Taxon O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: For example , the timing and synchrony of meiosis are very different in the two sexes .
label: O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: In females , germ cells synchronously initiate meiosis in the embryo and arrest during meiotic prophase I .
label: O O O B-CL I-CL O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: After puberty , oocytes are selectively recruited for ovulation , when they proceed to metaphase II and then complete meiosis after fertilization occurs [ 2 ] .
label: O O O B-CL O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: In contrast , male meiosis occurs entirely postnatally , without the arrest periods found in females .
label: O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: In females , each meiosis can produce a single haploid oocyte ( and two extruded polar bodies ) , whereas each male meiosis can produce four haploid spermatocytes .
label: O O O O O O O O O O B-CL O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-CL O

text_a: Other meiotic processes , such as recombination and chromosome pairing ( synapsis ) , occur in both sexes but operate somewhat differently .
label: O O O O O O O O B-GO O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: For example , there is a higher failure rate for meiosis in females , with human oocyte aneuploidy rates up to 25 % versus about 2 % in human sperm [ 3 ] , and this may indicate that the checkpoints controlling and monitoring the events of meiotic progression in males are more stringent .
label: O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-Taxon B-CL O O O O O O O O O O O B-Taxon B-CL O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: Consistent with this idea , genetic analysis of a number of meiotic regulatory genes in the mouse has demonstrated a much stronger requirement in males than in females [ 1 , 4 ] .
label: O O O O O B-SO O O O O O O O B-SO O O B-Taxon O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: The existence of heteromorphic sex chromosomes , such as the XX / XY system of mammals , creates sex - specific challenges .
label: O O O O B-GO I-GO O O O O O O O O O B-Taxon O O O O O O O

text_a: One is the need for mechanisms to balance expression of sex - linked genes between the sexes , which in mammals is accomplished by X chromosome inactivation in females [ 5 , 6 ] .
label: O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-SO O O O O O O B-Taxon O O O B-GO I-GO O O O O O O O O O

text_a: In male germ cells there is another sex - specific consideration during meiosis .
label: O B-CL I-CL I-CL O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: In prophase I , when the homologous chromosomes synapse and homologous recombination occurs , X and Y chromosome pairing is limited to a region termed the pseudoautosomal region , leaving large portions of each chromosome unpaired .
label: O O O O O O O B-GO O O O O O O B-GO I-GO I-GO I-GO O O O O O B-SO O O O B-SO O O O B-GO I-GO I-GO I-GO O O

text_a: In eutherian and marsupial mammals , these unpaired chromosome regions are associated with a specialized chromatin domain termed the XY body or sex body .
label: O B-Taxon I-Taxon I-Taxon I-Taxon O O O B-GO I-GO O O O O O B-GO I-GO O O B-GO I-GO O B-GO I-GO O

text_a: The function of the XY body is uncertain [ 7 - 11 ] , but there is evidence that it is essential for male meiotic progression [ 12 ] .
label: O O O O B-GO I-GO O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: Several proteins are reported to localize to the XY body , including BRCA1 , ATR , the histone variant H3 . 3 , and modified histones such as ubiquitinated H2A ( Ub - H2A ) and phosphorylated H2AX ( gamma H2AX ) [ 12 - 15 ] .
label: O B-CHEBI O O O O O O B-GO I-GO O O B-GGP O B-GGP O O B-CHEBI B-SO O O O O O B-SO B-CHEBI O O O O O O O O O O O B-GGP O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: In the XY body , the sex chromosomes are transcriptionally silenced in a process termed meiotic sex chromosome inactivation ( MSCI ) .
label: O O B-GO I-GO O O B-GO I-GO O O O O O O O O B-GO I-GO O O O O O

text_a: The XY body disappears after pachynema ; however , many sex - linked genes remain transcriptionally silent into spermiogenesis [ 16 ] .
label: O B-GO I-GO O O O O O O O O O O B-SO O O O O O O O O O

text_a: This maintenance of silencing is associated with a distinct set of chromatin marks that define a sex chromatin domain termed postmeiotic sex chromatin ( PMSC ) [ 16 , 17 ] .
label: O O O O O O O O O O O B-GO O O O O B-GO I-GO B-SO O O B-GO I-GO O O O O O O O O O

text_a: Regulators of sexual differentiation have been identified in a number of organisms , but in contrast to many other developmental processes , such as axial patterning or development of many body parts , the molecular mechanisms that regulate sexual differentiation are highly variable between phyla .
label: O O O O O O O O O O O B-Taxon O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-CHEBI O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: A notable exception involves genes related to doublesex ( dsx ) of Drosophila , which share a Doublesex / MAB - 3 DNA - binding motif called the DM domain [ 18 , 19 ] .
label: O O O O B-SO O O B-GGP I-GGP I-GGP I-GGP I-GGP I-GGP O O O O O O O O O B-SO O O O O O O B-SO O O O O O O

text_a: DM domain - encoding genes have been shown to regulate various aspects of sexual differentiation in insects , nematodes , and mammals [ 20 ] .
label: O B-SO O O B-SO O O O O O O O O O O O B-Taxon O B-Taxon O O B-Taxon O O O O

text_a: The mab - 3 gene of Caenorhabditis elegans has been shown to function analogously to DSX in several respects and can be functionally replaced by the male isoform of DSX , suggesting that the similarity in the sequence of these genes may stem from conservation of an ancestral DM domain sexual regulator [ 18 , 21 , 22 ] .
label: O B-GGP I-GGP I-GGP B-SO O B-Taxon I-Taxon O O O O O O O B-GGP O O O O O O O O O O O B-SO O B-GGP O O O O O O O B-SO O O B-SO O O O B-SO O O O O B-SO O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: Vertebrates also have DM domain genes , and analysis to date , although limited , has shown that these genes also control sexual differentiation .
label: B-Taxon O O O B-SO B-SO O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-SO O O O O O

text_a: Mammals have seven DM domain genes ( Dmrt genes ) , several of which exhibit sexually dimorphic mRNA expression [ 23 , 24 ] .
label: B-Taxon O O O B-SO B-SO O O B-SO O O O O O O O O B-CHEBI O O O O O O O

text_a: The best studied of these genes , Dmrt1 , is expressed in the differentiating male genital ridges and adult testis of mammals , birds , fish , and reptiles , and a recently duplicated Dmrt1 gene functions as the Y - linked testis - determining gene in the Medaka fish [ 25 - 29 ] .
label: O O O O O B-SO O B-GGP O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-Taxon O B-Taxon O B-Taxon O O B-Taxon O O O O O B-GGP B-SO O O O B-GO O O O O O B-SO O O B-Taxon I-Taxon O O O O O O

text_a: Human DMRT1 maps to an autosomal locus , which , when hemizygous , is associated with defective testicular development and consequent XY feminization [ 30 ] .
label: B-GGP I-GGP O O O B-GO B-SO O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: Similarly , mice homozygous for a nan mutation in Dmrt1 have severe defects in testis differentiation involving both germ cells and Sertoli cells [ 31 ] .
label: O O B-Taxon O O O O B-SO O B-GGP O O O O O O O O B-CL I-CL O B-CL I-CL O O O O

text_a: Female mice mutant in Dmrt4 have polyovular follicles , indicating that this gene also plays a role in gonadal development [ 32 ] .
label: O B-Taxon B-SO O B-GGP O O O O O O O B-SO O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: It appears from these studies that the involvement of DM domain genes in sexual differentiation is ancient and conserved .
label: O O O O O O O O O O B-SO B-SO O O O O O O O O

text_a: However , vertebrate Dmrt gene function is not limited to sexual differentiation : Dmrt2 is required in both sexes for segmentation in mice and fish [ 33 - 35 ] .
label: O O B-Taxon O B-SO O O O O O O O O B-GGP O O O O O O O O B-Taxon O B-Taxon O O O O O O

text_a: Here , we have investigated the expression and function of the Dmrt7 gene in the mouse .
label: O O O O O O O O O O O B-GGP B-SO O O B-Taxon O

text_a: Dmrt7 is expressed only in the gonad , and , unlike the other Dmrt genes , appears to be present exclusively in mammals and not in nonmammalian vertebrates [ 23 , 36 ] .
label: B-GGP O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-SO O O O O O O O B-Taxon O O O O B-Taxon O O O O O O

text_a: We find that DMRT7 protein is expressed only in germ cells and is selectively localized to the XY body of male pachytene germ cells .
label: O O O B-GGP B-CHEBI O O O O B-CL I-CL O O O O O O B-GO I-GO O B-CL I-CL I-CL I-CL O

text_a: To test its function , we generated a conditional nan mutation of Dmrt7 in the mouse .
label: O O O O O O O O O O B-SO O B-GGP O O B-Taxon O

text_a: We find that Dmrt7 is required in males for progression beyond the pachytene stage of meiotic prophase but is not required in females .
label: O O O B-GGP O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

label: O O B-SO B-CL O O O O O O O B-GO I-GO O O

text_a: Based on these observations , we suggest that Dmrt7 plays a critical role in a male - specific chromatin transition between pachynema and diplonema during meiotic prophase .

text_a: Monotremes have five X and five Y chromosomes , which form an extended pairing chain during meiosis and appear unrelated to the sex chromosomes of the other mammals [ 61 ] .
label: B-Taxon O O B-GO I-GO I-GO I-GO I-GO O O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-GO I-GO O O O B-Taxon O O O O

text_a: The presence of Dmrt7 in both lineages may support a common origin for either the sex chromosomes or the sex chromatin of monotremes and other mammals .
label: O O O B-GGP O O O O O O O O O O O B-GO I-GO O O B-GO I-GO O B-Taxon O O B-Taxon O

text_a: A plausible model is that Dmrt7 evolved during the establishment of mammalian sex determination to help cope with ancestral differences in gene dosage , chromosome pairing , recombination , or other meiotic issues arising from sex chromosome heteromorphy .
label: O O O O O B-GGP O O O O O B-Taxon O O O O O O O O O B-SO O O B-GO O O O O O O O O O O B-GO I-GO O O

text_a: In this regard , we speculate that the recruitment of Dmrt7 during mammalian evolution may be analogous to the recruitment of chromatin regulatory complexes to achieve somatic dosage compensation during evolution of heteromorphic sex chromosomes in several phyla ( reviewed in [ 62 ] ) .
label: O O O O O O O O O O B-GGP O B-Taxon O O O O O O O O B-GO O B-GO O O O O O O O O O B-GO I-GO O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: It will be of interest to determine whether DMRT7 localizes to sex chromosomes during monotreme meiosis .
label: O O O O O O O O B-GGP O O B-GO I-GO O B-Taxon O O

text_a: In summary , we have found that the mammal - specific DM domain protein DMRT7 is essential for meiotic prophase progression in males .
label: O O O O O O O O B-Taxon O O O B-SO B-CHEBI B-GGP O O O O O O O O O

text_a: DMRT7 localizes to the sex chromosomes after they are assembled into specialized heterochromatin , and many Dmrt7 mutant cells have epigenetic defects in the modification of the sex chromatin between pachytene and diplotene .
label: B-GGP O O O B-GO I-GO O O O O O O B-GO O O O B-GGP B-SO B-CL O O O O O B-SO O O B-GO I-GO O O O O O

text_a: Although Dmrt7 belongs to an ancient and conserved gene family , it is found only in mammals , and to our knowledge DMRT7 is the only example of a mammal - specific protein that is essential for meiosis .
label: O B-GGP O O O O O B-SO B-SO O O O O O O O B-Taxon O O O O O B-GGP O O O O O O B-Taxon O O B-CHEBI O O O O O O

text_a: It will be important to determine the precise mechanism by which DMRT7 affects sex chromatin regulation during male meiosis .

text_a: In mammalian meiosis , homologous chromosome synapsis is coupled with recombination .

text_a: As in most eukaryotes , mammalian meiocytes have checkpoints that monitor the fidelity of these processes .

text_a: We report that the mouse ortholog ( Trip13 ) of pachytene checkpoint 2 ( PCH2 ) , an essential component of the synapsis checkpoint in Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Caenorhabditis elegans , is required for completion of meiosis in both sexes .

text_a: Author Summary

text_a: It is critical that the chromosomes carried by sperm and eggs contain faithful representations of the genome of the individual that produced them .
label: O O O O O B-GO O O B-CL O B-CL O O O O O B-SO O O B-Taxon O O O O

text_a: During the process of meiosis , the maternal and paternal copies of each chromosome synapse with each other ( become tightly associated ) , exchange genetic material via the process of recombination , then separate into daughter cells in the first of two meiotic cell divisions .
label: O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-GO O O O O O O O O O O O B-SO B-SO O O O O O O O O O O B-CL O O O O O O B-CL O O

text_a: The intricate chromosome behavior is subject to errors , so most organisms have evolved meiotic checkpoints that monitor fidelity of chromosome synapsis and repair of DNA damage .
label: O O B-GO O O O O O O O O B-Taxon O O O O O O O O B-GO O O O O B-SO O O

text_a: These checkpoints cause defective cells to self destruct rather than generate defective sperm or eggs .
label: O O O O B-CL O O O O O O O B-CL O B-CL O

text_a: We studied the effects of deleting mouse Trip13 , a gene that in distant organisms plays a key role in meiotic checkpoint control .
label: O O O O O B-SO B-GGP I-GGP O O B-SO O O O B-Taxon O O O O O O O O O

text_a: These experiments revealed that instead of having a checkpoint role , Trip13 is required for one of the two major classes of recombination in meiosis that is required for repairing broken DNA molecules .

text_a: Introduction
label: O

text_a: The genesis of gametes containing an intact , haploid genome is critical for the prevention of birth defects , and is highly dependent upon the fidelity of chromosome dynamics before the first meiotic division .
label: O O O B-CL O O O O O B-SO O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-GO O O O O O O O

text_a: Homologous chromosomes must pair , synapse , undergo recombination , and segregate properly to opposite poles .
label: O B-GO O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: Recombination , which repairs repair double strand breaks ( DSBs ) that are genetically induced in leptonema , is coupled with synapsis in budding yeast and mammals .
label: O O O O O B-SO I-SO O O O O O O B-SO O O O O O O O O O B-Taxon I-Taxon O B-Taxon O

text_a: While our knowledge of the assembly and nature of recombination machinery is extensive , little is known about the disassembly of recombination intermediates , recruitment of DNA replication machinery during recombinational repair , and how the choice between different repair pathways is made .
label: O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-SO O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: Defects in recombination can preclude homologous chromosome pairing , leave unrepaired chromosome breaks , and cause aneuploidy by abrogating crossing over .
label: O O O O O O B-GO O O O O B-GO O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: To avoid such deleterious outcomes , surveillance systems ( checkpoints ) exist to sense meiotic errors and eliminate cells containing unresolved defects .
label: O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-CL O O O O

text_a: In many organisms , including S . cerevisiae , Drosophila melanogaster , C . elegans , and mice [ 1 - 4 ] , meiocytes with defects in recombination and / or chromosome synapsis trigger meiotic arrest in the pachytene stage of meiotic prophase I .
label: O O B-Taxon O O B-Taxon I-Taxon I-Taxon O B-Taxon I-Taxon O B-Taxon I-Taxon I-Taxon O O B-Taxon O O O O O O O O O O O O O O B-GO O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: This response to meiotic defects is referred to as the pachytene checkpoint ( reviewed in [ 5 ] ) .
label: O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: Genetic experiments in S . cerevisiae have identified elements of the pachytene checkpoint machinery ( reviewed in [ 5 ] ) .
label: B-SO O O B-Taxon I-Taxon I-Taxon O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O O

text_a: In addition to meiosis - specific proteins , these include factors that play roles in DNA damage signaling in mitotic cells [ 6 - 10 ] .
label: O O O O O O B-CHEBI O O O O O O O O B-SO O O O O B-CL O O O O O O

text_a: Arabidopsis thaliana does not appear to have a pachytene checkpoint akin to that in yeast [ 11 ] , nor do male Drosophila .
label: B-Taxon I-Taxon O O O O O O O O O O O O B-Taxon O O O O O O O B-Taxon O

text_a: The pachytene checkpoint is known to monitor two aspects of meiotic chromosome metabolism in S . cerevisiae and C . elegans : ( 1 ) DSB repair and ( 2 ) chromosome synapsis [ 2 , 12 ] .
label: O O O O O O O O O O O B-GO O O B-Taxon I-Taxon I-Taxon O B-Taxon I-Taxon I-Taxon O O O O O O O O O O B-GO O O O O O O O

text_a: In mice , both spermatocytes and oocytes harboring mutations that disrupt DSB repair ( such as Dmc1 , Msh5 , and Atm ) are efficiently eliminated in pachynema , but spermatocytes are much more sensitive to DSB repair - independent synapsis defects than oocytes [ 13 - 15 ] .
label: O B-Taxon O O B-CL O B-CL O B-SO O O O O O O O B-GGP O B-GGP O O B-GGP O O O O O O O O B-CL O O O O O O O O O O O O B-CL O O O O O O

text_a: However , because recombination is required for synapsis in mice ( mutations in recombination genes such as Dmc1 cause extensive asynapsis [ 16 ] ) , it has remained formally uncertain whether there is a distinct pachytene checkpoint that responds to defects in meiotic recombination , and if so , whether it would be identical to that used in somatic cells .
